claimText: GlowCas9 is a luminescent CRISPR protein developed by the Bose Institute that emits light during gene editing.
evidenceExcerpt: GlowCas9 was developed by scientists at the Bose Institute (DST). It is a CRISPR protein fused with nano-luciferase fragments from deep-sea shrimp that emits light when performing gene editing.
examUse: Statement-based question on recent indigenous biotechnology developments.
distractorHooks: Used for protein synthesis instead of gene editing

claimText: The luminescent property of GlowCas9 is achieved by fusing a CRISPR protein with nano-luciferase fragments derived from deep-sea shrimp.
evidenceExcerpt: It is a CRISPR protein fused with nano-luciferase fragments from deep-sea shrimp that emits light when performing gene editing.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching biotech tools with their biological sources.

claimText: Epigenome editing regulates gene expression by modifying the epigenome without altering the underlying DNA sequence.
evidenceExcerpt: Epigenome editing regulates gene expression by modifying the epigenome without changing the actual DNA sequence.
examUse: Comparison question distinguishing epigenome editing from traditional gene editing techniques that alter DNA sequences.
distractorHooks: Involves the insertion of foreign DNA
Causes permanent mutations in the genetic code
